reasoning: The question asks if Kilmar Abrego Garcia will return to the United States before July 1, 2025. He was wrongfully deported in March 2025 to a prison in El Salvador. Despite court orders, including from the Supreme Court, to facilitate his return, both the Trump administration and Salvadoran President Nayib Bukele are vehemently opposed to it. As of April 30, 2025, Secretary of State Marco Rubio publicly refused to comply with the court orders, and President Bukele outright rejected diplomatic requests to return him, stating his administration does not release accused gang members. The Trump administration considers him a gang member. The U.S. executive branch is openly defying judicial orders to return him, and even if they wanted to, El Salvador currently holds jurisdiction over his physical custody and is refusing to release him. Given that only two months remain until the July 1, 2025 deadline and the absolute intransigence from both foreign and domestic executive branches, the probability of him returning in this timeline is extremely low. Even an unlikely scenario of contempt of court charges moving things along would take far longer than two months. The only way it could happen would be if an unforeseen political deal is brokered, which the latest news explicitly denies. I confidently put the probability near the floor allowed by the prompt.
